ClassMarkers.description:
Container for a class entry in sidecars.

Class-scoped markers apply to every method of the class. Per-method
overrides go under ``tests.<ClassName>.<method>`` (qualified form)
rather than nesting inside the class block.
ClassMarkers.title: ClassMarkers

MarkerSpec.description:
One entry in a ``markers:`` list — mirrors a pytest decorator.

Four YAML shapes are parsed by :meth:`from_raw`:

==================================== =============================================
YAML                                 Parsed to
==================================== =============================================
``- flaky``                          ``MarkerSpec(name="flaky")``
``- skip: "reason"``                 ``MarkerSpec(name="skip", args=["reason"])``
``- parametrize: ["vin", [1, 2]]``   ``MarkerSpec(name="parametrize",
                                         args=["vin", [1, 2]])``
``- litmus_limits: {v_rail: {...}}`` ``MarkerSpec(name="litmus_limits",
                                         kwargs={"v_rail": {...}})``
==================================== =============================================

List payloads expand to positional args; dict payloads are keyword
args; string/number/bool payloads become a single positional arg;
bare names have neither. Mirrors how pytest decorators are called,
so a reader who knows ``@pytest.mark.parametrize(...)`` can read the
YAML directly.
MarkerSpec.title: MarkerSpec

OutputConfig.description:
A single output entry in the ``outputs`` list.

Each entry specifies a format (exporter), a transport, or both:

.. code-block:: yaml

    outputs:
      - format: html                    # report only
      - format: csv                     # export only
      - format: stdf
        transport: s3                   # export + ship
        bucket: my-results
      - transport: snowflake            # ship Parquet directly

Extra keys (bucket, server, dsn_env, template, etc.) are passed
through as format- or transport-specific configuration.

Note: ``format`` and ``transport`` names are not validated against the
registries at config time (registries are lazy-loaded). Invalid names
will raise ``KeyError`` at runtime when the output is executed.
OutputConfig.title: OutputConfig

ProfileConfig.description:
A named config set applied to a pytest session.

Profiles carry pytest markers at three scopes: file-wide ``markers``
(applied to every test), ``classes.<ClassName>.markers`` (applied
to every method of a class), and ``tests.<name>.markers`` (per-test).
Shape matches the sidecar ``SidecarConfig`` so one vocabulary
spans inline decorators, sidecar YAML, and profile overrides.

``extends`` names another profile whose configuration is inherited and
overridden last-wins by this one. Chains are walked parent-first, so a
family / platform base can declare the shared 90% and each leaf profile
holds only deltas. Parent profiles with no ``facets`` are reachable
only as extends targets.
ProfileConfig.title: ProfileConfig

ProfilePytest.description:
Pytest-level knobs a profile can apply.

``addopts`` is appended to ``PYTEST_ADDOPTS`` before collection so
downstream plugins (pytest-rerunfailures, pytest-xdist, pytest-timeout)
parse it during their own configure phase.
ProfilePytest.title: ProfilePytest

TestMarkers.description: Container for a test / class entry in sidecars and profiles.
TestMarkers.title: TestMarkers

description: Schema for litmus.yaml project config files — all fields at root.
